title: Entity Registry (seed) — people -> roles
description: TEMPLATE, not data. The people->roles single-source-of-truth the original instance never built as a standalone artifact (ARCHITECTURE.md §7) — so it is the FIRST artifact a new instance seeds. Mechanism only: every value below is a {company-slot} marker; the filled instance copy is `company_profile.entity_registry` in a manifest.json validated against tooling/config.schema.json, which accepts ONLY a `people` array. COMPANIES are NOT seeded here — they are DERIVED from the <projects_root>/ folder names, and any company metadata a folder name can't carry (an alias, a role label, the canonical Overview path) lives in that company's Overview.md frontmatter, which the entity-resolver folds into the project graph.
x-seed-order: FIRST — seed before any content load; an unresolved person is the root cause of the recurring entity tangles the framework inverts.

people.description: MANDATORY seed. One row per known person (own-company staff + recurring external contacts). Fills company_profile.entity_registry.people. The entity-resolver C module resolves names/emails seen in email + meetings against this list; the focus-detector EXTENDS it per person via person_profile.focus.extra_entities (never overwrites it).

email.description: {person-email} — primary address; the key the email adapter resolves senders against.

internal.description: True for own-company staff (matches company_profile.company.domain). Drives internal-vs-external sender classification; an external contact's employing org is inferred from their email domain + the project graph, not stored here.

_examples.description: NOT part of the registry — illustration only. An instance DELETES this key and fills `people` for real. (Company metadata is not seeded here — annotate it in the company's Overview.md frontmatter; companies are derived from their folders.)
